name: apply_deformer_with_vmap_mask
description: Verify the canonical creative loop: paint a radial vmap (radius=200 to actually hit cube surface verts), then apply a Bend deformer restricted to it. Confirms (a) vmap painting writes a non-uniform gradient, (b) deformer is added as child by type_id, (c) restriction tag is bound to the vmap.

args.script:
import c4d
from c4d import utils
doc = c4d.documents.GetActiveDocument()
for n in ['RecipeCube']:
    o = doc.SearchObject(n)
    if o: o.Remove()
cg = c4d.BaseObject(c4d.Ocube)
cg[c4d.PRIM_CUBE_LEN] = c4d.Vector(200, 200, 200)
cg[c4d.PRIM_CUBE_SUBX] = 10
cg[c4d.PRIM_CUBE_SUBY] = 10
cg[c4d.PRIM_CUBE_SUBZ] = 10
doc.InsertObject(cg)
res = utils.SendModelingCommand(c4d.MCOMMAND_MAKEEDITABLE, [cg], doc=doc)
p = res[0]
p.SetName('RecipeCube')
doc.InsertObject(p)
c4d.EventAdd()
print('setup ok, points=' + str(p.GetPointCount()))

import c4d
doc = c4d.documents.GetActiveDocument()
o = doc.SearchObject('RecipeCube')
if o: o.Remove()
c4d.EventAdd()
print('teardown ok')
